rulings.Case Title: TAL N. TAMIR ET AL VS. LEM HOLDINGS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOVING PARTY: APR REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC., COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC., PAULA ROSE; DEFENDANTS APR REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC. DBA COMPASS; COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC. DBA COMPASS AND PAULA Y. ROSE'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN ORDER GRANTING LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 14, 2023, line 1. Defendant APR Real Estates' Motion for Leave to File a Cross-Complaint is continued to June 16, 2023 to be heard with other pending motions. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TRUE GOSPEL MISSIONARY BAPTIST CHURCH VS. TY INVESTMENT LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summ Adjudiction
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 14, 2023, line 2. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Notice of Motion and the Separate Statement do not comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. There are multiple triable issues of fact. See Plaintiff's Response to Defendant's Additional Material Facts. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: BAYMARK FINANCIAL, INC. VS. SALLY L. LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Pltf Baymark Financial, Inc.'S Complaint For Failure To Timely File An Amended Complaint After Demurrer Was Sustained With Leave To Amend
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 14, 2023, line 5. Defendant's Motion to Dismiss Complaint is continued to August 4, 2023. Plaintiff to file and serve a properly noticed CCP 473(b) motion and notice it for hearing on August 4, 2023. In the motion Plaintiff's counsel to address why an attempt to file an amended complaint was made on May 4, 2023 (after April 26, 2023 deadline as conceded in the opposition to this motion) without leave of Court and why CCP 473(b) motion has not been filed as soon as Plaintiff's counsel realized their calendaring mistake. Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear. =(501/CFH)
